title: Kerala Mulls Green Hydrogen Policy
content:
The Kerala government is working on a green hydrogen policy, with a draft prepared by ANERT (Agency for New and Renewable Energy Research & Technology) and submitted for approval. The policy is expected to be finalized soon and aims to help Kerala achieve carbon neutrality by 2050. Under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, Kerala has secured approval for a â€œHydrogen Valley Innovation Clusterâ€ project worth â‚¹133.18 crore. In its pilot phase, the project targets producing 57 tonnes of green hydrogen.

Pilot applications envisioned include heavy road transport, houseboats, and other mobility sectors. Though green hydrogen remains costly, the government considers it essential to reducing carbon emissions. The draft policy signals Keralaâ€™s intent to position itself among leading green hydrogen states through both innovation and implementation.

Keralaâ€™s status as a nodal renewable energy state, and its ambitious target of becoming fully renewable by 2040, means green hydrogen could play a pivotal role. By combining policy, pilot projects, and sectoral deploymentâ€”especially in transport and maritime sectorsâ€”the draft aims to catalyze domestic investment, spur green energy innovation, and align with both state and national climate goals.
author: Analysts Account
